verdict: 2026-07-14 08:12:40 PDT — The proposed component shape is modern, minimal, and consistent with the repository: it reuses CostOverlay, sessionControls, and existing state rather than adding abstractions or dependencies. It is ready once the wording contract is resolved and the spinner CSS is explicitly scoped.

findings.title: Required copy references a missing decision
findings.claim: AC5 requires exact wording from a “Design decisions” section that does not exist, while the wording remains an open question. The sketch therefore does not uniquely specify the labels and hint that the implementation will be reviewed against.
findings.alternative: Have Thomas confirm the recommended wording, then promote it into a Design decisions section and remove the corresponding open question.
findings.win: Creates one objective copy contract and eliminates an otherwise subjective acceptance decision during implementation review.

findings.title: Spinner reset should not target every number input
findings.claim: The sketched WebKit selectors are global (`input::-webkit-outer-spin-button` and `input::-webkit-inner-spin-button`). The surrounding stylesheet contains other number-input contexts, so implementing the sketch literally would remove their steppers too, despite AC3 targeting only the two panel fields.
findings.alternative: Scope all reset selectors to the presenter component, for example `.num-input input[type='number']` and `.num-input input[type='number']::-webkit-*`.
findings.win: Satisfies AC3 without changing unrelated input affordances or creating a hidden cross-panel styling rule.
